title: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
content: Rabiu Kwankwaso joins the ADC, a coalition poised to take on President Tinubu in the 2027 elections.

originalTitle: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
title: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
content: Locals say the structure, in a school complex, was in use despite lying unfinished for a number of years.

originalTitle: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
content: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

originalTitle: US raises concern over Israel’s closure of Jerusalem holy sites
title: US raises concern over Israel’s closure of Jerusalem holy sites
content: The White House says the US has raised concerns with Israel over closure of Jerusalem holy sites.

originalTitle: Iran’s foreign ministry rejects US ceasefire demands
title: Iran’s foreign ministry rejects US ceasefire demands
content: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says US conditions for a ceasefire are ‘excessive, unrealistic and unreasonable’.

originalTitle: Key moments from Marco Rubio’s interview with Al Jazeera
title: A Friendly Talk for Peace: News from Far Away!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends from across Africa! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter, here to tell you about some important news from far, far away. Today, we're going to talk about a very important leader and how countries try to be friends.

Hello from Far Away!

Imagine you have a big family, and maybe sometimes someone in your family has a little problem or disagreement with another person. Well, countries are like super-duper big families! And just like people, sometimes countries have big talks about how to get along and be safe. That’s what our news story is about today. We heard about a very important person named Mr. Marco. He is from a big country called the United States of America, or the USA for short. The USA is very far from us, across a big ocean! Mr. Marco is a big leader in his country. He helps make important decisions, just like your parents or teachers help you make good choices every day. Mr. Marco had a special talk on a news channel called Al Jazeera. A news channel is like a TV station that tells everyone what is happening around the world. Another kind person, named Mr. Hashem, asked Mr. Marco lots of questions. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 amazing countries! Each one is like a unique family with its own stories and dreams.

Meeting a Big Leader

So, who is Mr. Marco, exactly? He is a very important person who works for the government of the USA. His job is to help his country talk to other countries. Think of him like a special messenger or a kind uncle who helps everyone get along. He works hard to make sure his country stays safe and has good friends around the world. During his talk, Mr. Marco spoke about another country called Iran. Iran is also far away from us, in a part of the world called Asia. Just like the USA, Iran is a big country with lots of people, homes, and schools. Sometimes, countries can have big disagreements, like when two friends might argue over a toy or a game. Leaders like Mr. Marco try to solve these disagreements. They want to make sure that everyone, especially children, can live in a safe and happy world. It’s like when your teacher helps two friends sort out a problem – they talk, they listen, and they try to find a way to make everyone happy again. Did you know? Leaders of countries are sometimes called presidents, prime ministers, or kings! Their main job is to help their people and their country.

Countries Are Like Big Families

When we talk about countries, we mean a big place on the map with its own people, rules, and leaders. It’s like your town or village, but much, much bigger! And just like how families want to be happy, countries want their people to be happy and safe too. But sometimes, like in any big family, there can be tough times or disagreements. This news story is about the USA and Iran, two countries that are very far apart. They have different ways of doing things, and sometimes this can cause big problems. But instead of letting problems grow, leaders want to talk. They want to find ways to understand each other better, so everyone can live in peace. Even though these countries are very far away, news helps us learn about them. It helps us understand that we all share the same big planet, and it's important for everyone to try and get along. Imagine if everyone in your school decided to be kind and talk through problems – how wonderful would that be? Did you know? The world has nearly 200 different countries! Each one has unique customs, foods, and languages, which makes our world so colorful and interesting!

Talking About Being Friends

Mr. Marco talked a lot about finding peaceful ways to solve problems. He explained that his country, the USA, wants to talk things through with Iran. Talking is like using your words to explain how you feel and to listen to what others say. It’s much better than shouting or being unkind, isn’t it? He wants to make sure that all countries respect each other. Respect means being kind and understanding, even if you don't always agree. He said that by talking, countries can avoid big troubles and make sure everyone feels safe and secure in their homes and their lands. This is a very important message for all of us. Imagine if you could solve all your problems just by talking and listening. That's what grown-up leaders try to do for their countries. They want to protect their people and find ways for everyone to live happily side-by-side. It takes a lot of patience and understanding, but it’s the best way. Did you know? Talking is always better than fighting! It helps us learn, grow, and make new friends.

Why Talking Is So Important

Why is all this talking so important? Well, when countries talk, they learn about each other. They can share their ideas and find solutions that work for everyone. It stops big problems from getting even bigger. It helps to build bridges of friendship instead of walls of disagreement. Mr. Marco stressed that finding solutions means everyone must be willing to listen and find common ground. Common ground means finding things that you both agree on, even if you have different ideas about other things. It's like when you and a friend both love playing outside, even if you like different games. He wants a future where children in the USA, children in Iran, and children all over the world can grow up without worrying about big country problems. He wants them to play, learn, and dream big dreams in peace and safety. That's a beautiful wish, don't you think? Did you know? A special word for people whose job is to talk between countries to solve problems is 'diplomat'! They are super talkers!

Hoping for Peace for Everyone

So, the main message from Mr. Marco's talk is about hope for peace. Peace means no fighting, no sadness, and everyone feeling calm and happy. It means everyone can go to school, play with their friends, and sleep soundly at night without any worries. This is what all good leaders want for their people. Mr. Marco hopes that through these important talks, the USA and Iran can find a way to live together in peace. He hopes they can be good neighbors, even though they are far apart. And we, here in Africa, also hope for peace for everyone, everywhere! Because when there is peace, everyone wins! It’s wonderful to know that leaders are working hard to make our world a safer and happier place for all children, including you! Let's all remember to talk things through, be kind, and hope for peace in our own homes, schools, and communities, just like Mr. Marco hopes for peace for the world. Did you know? The white dove is a very old and special symbol of peace all around the world! When you see a picture of a dove, it often means peace and hope.

originalTitle: Spain closes airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran war
title: Spain closes airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran war
content: It follows a decision by the Spanish government to deny the US use of the two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.

title: Russian oil tanker reaches Cuba after Trump appears to loosen blockade
content: The tanker has reached Cuban waters just hours after Donald Trump said he had "no problem" with it.

title: Zelensky says allies asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy
content: Ukraine's president says partners asked if the attacks could be "reduced" as prices soar due to the Iran war.

title: De Zerbi in advanced talks to become Spurs manager
content: Tottenham Hotspur enter into advanced talks with Roberto de Zerbi, with the former Brighton manager now willing to take charge immediately, according to sources.

title: How Sky can ignore limit on its TV picks & why fans are fed up
content: Premier League games being moved to Friday or Monday are a headache for the fans of some clubs. Is the system fair, and how does it work for Sky?

originalTitle: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
title: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
content: Uefa is to expected to broadly freeze prices at Euro 2028 - meaning supporters would be able to buy five tickets for the cost of one parking space at a World Cup stadium this summer.
